generatedSentence: Elara, a refulgent wanderer, often felt her life lacked true purpose despite her masterful skills as a mixologist. Her flat in uptown, filled with unique antique articles and the tangy scent of ginger, seemed to offer little more than a steady line of customers, yet she maintained her dignity. She often enjoyed a quiet breakfast, observing the vast urbanization and panorama of obelisks from her atrium window, feeling a prodigy of wasted potential. Her current boyfriend, a quiet goaltender, found her interesting, even when she seemed resigned. One day, an antiquary in a peculiar jacket mentioned a cache of ancient fruits, rumored to unify lost civilizations; a neologism in her routine that promised a new venture. The antiquary’s words, a sound of promise, had deeply ticked Elara. She embarked on a hiking vacation, leaving her flat for the wilderness, intending to keep up with the legend. The journey was no picnic; she faced a nasty trader who dealt in exotic fruits and jam, and her lack of a military escort or any proper payment for information meant she often had to adapt quickly. Her computer, usually her outlet, now felt useless. She discovered a hidden university research facility beneath a jagged dome, where a massive whale-like computer hummed, its motherboard glowing. Inside, a grouchy postman, doubling as a security guard, oversaw the testing of unusual syringes, an activity that promised shocking revelations about the fruits. She felt a growing sense of dread, fearing a looming catastrophe. Diligence was paramount as Elara snuck into the basement, an underground kiln humming with bizarre energy. She discovered a clandestine ritual to vaporize the ancient fruits, turning them into flakes that sustained a secret society, a new form of indenture. She saw rockets being prepared, slated to release this potent dust over the entire city, leading to obliteration rather than greatness. She realized the king of this cult, a monstrous figure, intended to erase all individuality, to bonk anybody into submission, turning them into mere suckers for his cult's power. It was an ultimate versus, a fight for all kinship. She found herself in a desperate pocket, recognizing the horrifying overview of their plan: to drown the world in their mind-numbing jaggery. Elara, an astronaut of willpower, knew she had to finish what she started. She remembered the kudos her gallantry had earned in the past. With a precise tap of a pen on the dashboard, she overloaded the cult's systems, releasing a cascade of lavender light and erasing the destructive data. The cult’s operations were destructed, their treadmill of power brought to a halt. As the power flickered and the cultists scattered, their loots and nasty plans dissolved. Elara had orchestrated a masterful win, a victory for freedom. She even found a lone avocado, a cute symbol of renewal amidst the chaos, a mormon blessing. The world was safe, and Elara, the wanderer, had finally found her greatness, securing a future free from their cult's sinister notches.

Her flat in uptown, filled with unique antique articles and the tangy scent of ginger, seemed to offer little more than a steady line of customers, yet she maintained her dignity. She often enjoyed a quiet breakfast, observing the vast urbanization and panorama of obelisks from her atrium window, feeling a prodigy of wasted potential. Her current boyfriend, a quiet goaltender, found her interesting, even when she seemed resigned. One day, an antiquary in a peculiar jacket mentioned a cache of ancient fruits, rumored to unify lost civilizations; a neologism in her routine that promised a new venture.

The antiquary’s words, a sound of promise, had deeply ticked Elara. She embarked on a hiking vacation, leaving her flat for the wilderness, intending to keep up with the legend. The journey was no picnic; she faced a nasty trader who dealt in exotic fruits and jam, and her lack of a military escort or any proper payment for information meant she often had to adapt quickly. Her computer, usually her outlet, now felt useless. She discovered a hidden university research facility beneath a jagged dome, where a massive whale-like computer hummed, its motherboard glowing. Inside, a grouchy postman, doubling as a security guard, oversaw the testing of unusual syringes, an activity that promised shocking revelations about the fruits. She felt a growing sense of dread, fearing a looming catastrophe.

Diligence was paramount as Elara snuck into the basement, an underground kiln humming with bizarre energy. She saw rockets being prepared, slated to release this potent dust over the entire city, leading to obliteration rather than greatness. She realized the king of this cult, a monstrous figure, intended to erase all individuality, to bonk anybody into submission, turning them into mere suckers for his cult's power. It was an ultimate versus, a fight for all kinship. She found herself in a desperate pocket, recognizing the horrifying overview of their plan: to drown the world in their mind-numbing jaggery.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A female protagonist, Elara, wearing a refulgent lavender space suit and a unique chrome helmet with a glowing green visor. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Elara stands in her futuristic, high-rise flat, looking out at a sprawling neon cityscape at sunrise, a wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Elara cautiously enters a hidden, overgrown university research facility entrance, a medium shot from a low angle. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Elara confronts a monstrous, shadowy king figure in a glowing, futuristic basement kiln filled with strange machinery and rockets, a dynamic dutch angle. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Elara stands victorious amidst the remnants of destructed machinery, a single avocado plant sprouting from the rubble, a medium close-up, looking determined.

definitions.definition: To be without, to need, to require.
definitions.example: My life lacks excitement.

definitions.definition: To be short (of or for something).
definitions.example: He'll never lack for company while he's got all that money.

definitions.definition: To be in want.
To see the deficiency in (someone or something); to find fault with, to malign, reproach.
Having a sharp, pungent flavor
A V-shaped cut.
An indentation.
A mountain pass; a defile.
A discontinuous change in a taxation schedule.
A level or degree.
definitions.example: Can you speak a notch louder, please?

definitions.definition: A portion of a mobile phone that overlaps the edge of the screen, used to house camera, sensors etc. while maximizing screen space.
To cut a notch in (something).
To record (a score or similar) by making notches on something.
definitions.example: The tribe's hunters notch their kills by notches on each's axe's handle.

definitions.definition: To join by means of notches.
To achieve (something); to add to one's score or record of successes.
definitions.example: The team notched a pair of shutout wins on Sunday.

definitions.definition: To fit (an arrow) to a bow by means of the notch cut at the end of the arrow; to nock.
One who wanders, who travels aimlessly.
Any of various far-migrating nymphalid butterflies of the genus Danaus.
The wandering albatross, Diomedea exulans.
(often in the plural) In general, a product of plant growth useful to man or animals.

Specifically, a sweet, edible part of a plant that resembles seed-bearing fruit (see next sense), even if it does not develop from a floral ovary; also used in a technically imprecise sense for some sweet or sweetish vegetables, such as the petioles of rhubarb, that resemble a true fruit or are used in cookery as if they were a fruit.
A product of fertilization in a plant, specifically:
An end result, effect, or consequence; advantageous or disadvantageous result.
definitions.example: His long nights in the office eventually bore fruit when his business boomed and he was given a raise.

definitions.definition: Of, belonging to, related to, or having fruit or its characteristics; (of living things) producing or consuming fruit.
definitions.example: the fresh-squeezed fruit juice; a fruit salad; an artificial fruit flavor; a fruit tree; a fruit bat

definitions.definition: A homosexual man; an effeminate man.
Offspring from a sexual union.
definitions.example: Blessed art thou amongst women and blessed is the fruit of thy womb.

definitions.definition: To produce fruit, seeds, or spores.
A store of things that may be required in the future, which can be retrieved rapidly, protected or hidden in some way.
definitions.example: Members of the 29-man Discovery team laid down food caches to allow the polar team to travel light, hopping from food cache to food cache on their return journey.

definitions.definition: A fast temporary storage where recently or frequently used information is stored to avoid having to reload it from a slower storage medium.
(geocaching) A container containing treasure in a global treasure-hunt game.
To place in a cache.
To turn into vapor.
A person or animal that sucks, especially a breast or udder; especially a suckling animal, young mammal before it is weaned.
An undesired stem growing out of the roots or lower trunk of a shrub or tree, especially from the rootstock of a grafted plant or tree.
(by extension) A parasite; a sponger.
An organ or body part that does the sucking; especially a round structure on the bodies of some insects, frogs, and octopuses that allows them to stick to surfaces.
A thing that works by sucking something.
The embolus, or bucket, of a pump; also, the valve of a pump basket.
A pipe through which anything is drawn.

A small piece of leather, usually round, having a string attached to the center, which, when saturated with water and pressed upon a stone or other body having a smooth surface, adheres, by reason of the atmospheric pressure, with such force as to enable a considerable weight to be thus lifted by the string; formerly used by children as a plaything.
A suction cup.
An animal such as the octopus and remora, which adhere to other bodies with such organs.
Any fish in the family Catostomidae of North America and eastern Asia, which have mouths modified into downward-pointing, suckerlike structures for feeding in bottom sediments.
A lollipop; a piece of candy which is sucked.
A hard drinker.
An inhabitant of Illinois.

A migrant lead miner working in the Driftless Area of northwest Illinois, southwest Wisconsin, and northeast Iowa, working in summer and leaving for winter, so named because of the similarity to the migratory patterns of the North American Catostomidae.
A person who is easily deceived, tricked or persuaded to do something; a naive person.
definitions.example: One poor sucker had actually given her his life’s savings.

definitions.definition: A person irresistibly attracted by something specified.
definitions.example: A sucker for ghost stories.

definitions.definition: (British slang) The penis.
Any thing or object.
definitions.example: See if you can get that sucker working again.

definitions.definition: A person.
A brief summary, as of a book or a presentation.
An inspection.
To engage in an overview; to provide a brief summary.
A vent or similar passage to allow the escape of something.
Something which allows for the release of one's desires.
A river that runs out of a lake.
A shop that sells the products of a particular manufacturer or supplier.
A wall-mounted device such as a socket or receptacle connected to an electrical system at which current is taken to supply utilization equipment or appliances.
An earthenware container, either with two or no handles, for holding oil, water, wine, etc., or used for burial.
A small, approximately cylindrical container, normally made of clay or glass, for holding fruit, preserves, etc., or for ornamental purposes.
A jar and its contents; as much as fills such a container; a jarful.
To preserve (food) in a jar.
Cause to become one; make into a unit; consolidate; merge; combine.
Become one.
A thing without a like; something unequalled or unparallelled.
Being the only one of its kind; unequaled, unparalleled or unmatched.
definitions.example: Every person has a unique life, therefore every person has a unique journey. ― Gary Cook

definitions.definition: Of a feature, such that only one holder has it.
Particular, characteristic.
Of a rare quality, unusual.
A person who creates cocktails; a bartender.
A disc jockey.
The state of being dignified or worthy of esteem: elevation of mind or character.
Decorum, formality, stateliness.
High office, rank, or station.
One holding high rank; a dignitary.
Fundamental principle; axiom; maxim.
Something which is not genuine, or is presented fraudulently.
A trick; a swindle.
A move meant to deceive an opposing player, used for gaining advantage for example when dribbling an opponent.
One of the circles or windings of a cable or hawser, as it lies in a coil; a single turn or coil.
A loose filmy mass or a thin chiplike layer of anything
definitions.example: There were a few flakes of paint on the floor from when we were painting the walls.

definitions.definition: A scale of a fish or similar animal
A prehistoric tool chipped out of stone.
A person who is impractical, flighty, unreliable, or inconsistent; especially with maintaining a living.
definitions.example: She makes pleasant conversation, but she's kind of a flake when it comes time for action.

definitions.definition: A carnation with only two colours in the flower, the petals having large stripes.
A flat turn or tier of rope.
To break or chip off in a flake.
definitions.example: The paint flaked off after only a year.

definitions.definition: To prove unreliable or impractical; to abandon or desert, to fail to follow through.
definitions.example: He said he'd come and help, but he flaked.

definitions.definition: To store an item such as rope or sail in layers
definitions.example: The line is flaked into the container for easy attachment and deployment.

definitions.definition: To hit (another person).
A paling; a hurdle.
A platform of hurdles, or small sticks made fast or interwoven, supported by stanchions, for drying codfish and other things.
A small stage hung over a vessel's side, for workmen to stand on while calking, etc.
To make suitable; to make to correspond; to fit or suit
To fit by alteration; to modify or remodel for a different purpose; to adjust
definitions.example: to adapt a story for the stage

definitions.definition: To make by altering or fitting something else; to produce by change of form or character
definitions.example: a word of an adapted form

definitions.definition: To make oneself comfortable to a new thing.
definitions.example: They could not adapt to the new climate and so perished.

definitions.definition: Adapted; fit; suited; suitable.
The state, condition, or quality of being great
definitions.example: Due to the greatness of his size, he was an effective bodyguard.

definitions.definition: : Pride; haughtiness.
Courage
Chivalrous courtliness, especially towards women
An instance of gallant behaviour or speech
An instance of having won a competition or battle or succeeded in an effort.
definitions.example: It was a great victory on the battlefield.

definitions.definition: The condition of having succeeded in a conflict or other effort.
A winged figure representing victory, common in Roman official iconography. See Winged victory.
To achieve a victory
A tall, square, tapered, stone monolith topped with a pyramidal point, frequently used as a monument.

A symbol resembling a horizontal line (–), sometimes together with one or two dots (for example, ⨪ or ÷), which was used in ancient manuscripts and texts to mark a word or passage as doubtful or spurious, or redundant; an obelisk.

A dagger symbol (†), which is used in printed matter as a reference mark to refer the reader to a footnote, marginal note, etc.; beside a person's name to indicate that the person is deceased; or beside a date to indicate that it is a person's death date; an obelisk.
The total destruction of something.
The cancellation, erasure or deletion of something.

The cancellation of the function, structure, or both of a vessel or organ; for example, the occlusion of the lumen of a duct, blood vessel, or lymphatic vessel, be it solely functional (as when squeezed by nearby mass effect or inflammation) or both structural and functional (as when clogged with thrombus, embolus, or fibrosis).
A path through two or more points (compare ‘segment’); a continuous mark, including as made by a pen; any path, curved or straight.
definitions.example: The arrow descended in a curved line.

definitions.definition: A rope, cord, string, or thread, of any thickness.
A hose or pipe, of any size.
definitions.example: a brake line

definitions.definition: Direction, path.
definitions.example: the line of sight

definitions.definition: The wire connecting one telegraphic station with another, a telephone or internet cable between two points: a telephone or network connection.
definitions.example: I tried to make a call, but the line was dead.

definitions.definition: A clothesline.
A letter, a written form of communication.
definitions.example: Drop me a line.

definitions.definition: A connected series of public conveyances, as a roadbed or railway track; and hence, an established arrangement for forwarding merchandise, etc.
definitions.example: a line of stages

definitions.definition: A trench or rampart, or the non-physical demarcation of the extent of the territory occupied by specified forces.
The exterior limit of a figure or territory: a boundary, contour, or outline; a demarcation.
A long tape or ribbon marked with units for measuring; a tape measure.
A measuring line or cord.
That which was measured by a line, such as a field or any piece of land set apart; hence, allotted place of abode.
A threadlike crease or wrinkle marking the face, hand, or body; hence, a characteristic mark.
Lineament; feature; figure (of one's body).

A more-or-less straight sequence of people, objects, etc., either arranged as a queue or column and often waiting to be processed or dealt with, or arranged abreast of one another in a row (and contrasted with a column), as in a military formation.
definitions.example: The line forms on the right.

definitions.definition: The regular infantry of an army, as distinguished from militia, guards, volunteer corps, cavalry, artillery etc.
A series or succession of ancestors or descendants of a given person; a family or race; compare lineage.
A small amount of text. Specifically:
Course of conduct, thought, occupation, or policy; method of argument; department of industry, trade, or intellectual activity.
The official, stated position (or set of positions) of an individual or group, particularly a political or religious faction.
definitions.example: Remember, your answers must match the party line.

definitions.definition: A set of products or services sold by a business, or by extension, the business itself.
definitions.example: How many buses does the line have?

definitions.definition: A number of shares taken by a jobber.
A measure of length:
A maxwell, a unit of magnetic flux.
(1800s, with "the") The batter’s box.
The position in which the fencers hold their swords.
Proper relative position or adjustment (of parts, not as to design or proportion, but with reference to smooth working).
definitions.example: the engine is in line / out of line

definitions.definition: A small path-shaped portion or serving of a powdery illegal drug, especially cocaine.
Instruction; doctrine.
Population of cells derived from a single cell and containing the same genetic makeup.
(perfusion line) a set composed of a spike, a drip chamber, a clamp, a Y-injection site, a three-way stopcock and a catheter.
A group of forwards that play together.
A vascular catheter.
definitions.example: line sepsis

definitions.definition: To place (objects) into a line (usually used with "up"); to form into a line; to align.
definitions.example: to line troops

definitions.definition: To place persons or things along the side of for security or defense; to strengthen by adding; to fortify.
definitions.example: to line works with soldiers

definitions.definition: To form a line along.
To mark with a line or lines, to cover with lines.
definitions.example: to line a copy book

definitions.definition: To represent by lines; to delineate; to portray.
To read or repeat line by line.
definitions.example: to line out a hymn

definitions.definition: To hit a line drive; to hit a line drive which is caught for an out. Compare fly and ground.
definitions.example: Jones lined to left in his last at-bat.

definitions.definition: To track (wild bees) to their nest by following their line of flight.
To measure.
The process of the formation and growth of cities
The change in a country or region when its population migrates from rural to urban areas
The proportion of a region's population that live in towns and cities; the rate at which this proportion is growing
Of or pertaining to the manner of a master of an art, technique, profession, or craft; masterly.
definitions.example: The brushwork on the skin tones was masterful.

definitions.definition: In the manner of a master; very skillful.
definitions.example: The masterful conducting ensured the orchestra gave of their best.

definitions.definition: Having or showing the qualities of a master vis-a-vis a dependent or subordinate; authoritative; domineering.
(originally student slang) Irritable; easily upset; angry; tending to complain.
definitions.example: His boss gets grouchy when deadlines draw near.

definitions.definition: The pungent aromatic rhizome of a tropical Asian herb, Zingiber officinale, used as a spice and as a stimulant and acarminative.
The plant that produces this rhizome.
Other species belonging to the same family, Zingiberaceae, especially those of the genus Zingiber
A reddish-brown color.
A person with reddish-brown hair; a redhead.
Vitality, vigour, liveliness (of character).
To add ginger to.
To enliven, to spice (up).
To apply ginger to the anus of a horse to encourage it to carry its tail high and move in a lively fashion.
To inspire (someone); to give a little boost to.
(of hair) Of a reddish-brown colour.
The act of venerating or the state of being venerated.
Profound reverence, respect or awe.
Religious zeal, idolatry or devotion.
Healthy.
definitions.example: He was safe and sound.

definitions.definition: Complete, solid, or secure.
definitions.example: Fred assured me the floorboards were sound.

definitions.definition: Having the property of soundness.
Good; acceptable; decent.
definitions.example: "How are you?" - "I'm sound."

definitions.definition: (of sleep) Quiet and deep. Sound asleep means sleeping peacefully, often deeply.
definitions.example: Her sleep was sound.

definitions.definition: Heavy; laid on with force.
definitions.example: a sound beating

definitions.definition: Founded in law; legal; valid; not defective.
definitions.example: a sound title to land

definitions.definition: Soundly.
Yes; used to show agreement or understanding, generally without much enthusiasm.
One who earns a living by trading goods or securities.
Something nasty.
definitions.example: Processed foods are full of aspartame and other nasties.

definitions.definition: (preceded by "the") Sexual intercourse.
A video nasty.
Dirty, filthy.
Contemptible, unpleasant (of a person).
Objectionable, unpleasant (of a thing); repellent, offensive.
Indecent or offensive; obscene, lewd.
Spiteful, unkind.
Awkward, difficult to navigate; dangerous.
Grave or dangerous (of an accident, illness etc.).
Formidable, terrific; wicked.
To pull something sharply; to pull something out
definitions.example: She plucked the phone from her bag and dialled.

definitions.definition: To take or remove (someone) quickly from a particular place or situation.
To gently play a single string, e.g. on a guitar, violin etc.
definitions.example: Whereas a piano strikes the string, a harpsichord plucks it.

definitions.definition: To remove feathers from a bird.
To rob, fleece, steal forcibly
definitions.example: The horny highwayman plucked his victims to their underwear, or attractive ones all the way.

definitions.definition: To play a string instrument pizzicato.
definitions.example: Plucking a bow instrument may cause a string to break.

definitions.definition: To pull or twitch sharply.
definitions.example: to pluck at somebody's sleeve

definitions.definition: (university slang) To be rejected after failing an examination for a degree.
Of a glacier: to transport individual pieces of bedrock by means of gradual erosion through freezing and thawing.
(of something with feathers, hair etc.) Having had these items removed by plucking.
(of the strings of an instrument) Played by plucking.
Having courage and spirit; plucky.
Any large and disastrous event of great significance
A disaster beyond expectations
The dramatic event that initiates the resolution of the plot; the dénouement
A type of bifurcation, where a system shifts between two stable states
Rite; a repeated set of actions
definitions.example: They carried out the ritual carefully.

definitions.definition: Related to a rite or repeated set of actions.
definitions.example: They performed the ritual lighting of the candles.

definitions.definition: The residential part of a city, away from the commercial center
In the upper part of a town.
(by extension) Of or relating to an affluent area or population.
To or in the upper part of a town.
definitions.example: Let's go uptown and try out that new restaurant.

definitions.definition: To make a clicking noise similar to the movement of the hands in an analog clock.
To make a tick or checkmark.
To work or operate, especially mechanically.
definitions.example: He took the computer apart to see how it ticked.

definitions.definition: To strike gently; to pat.
To add a bird to a list of birds that have been seen (or heard).
To go on trust, or credit.
To give tick; to trust.
Ticked off; annoyed.
Armed forces.
definitions.example: He spent six years in the military.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of members of the armed forces.
definitions.example: She was dishonorably discharged from all military duties.

definitions.definition: Relating to armed forces such as the army, marines, navy and air force (often as distinguished from civilians or police forces).
definitions.example: If you join a military force, you may end up killing people.

definitions.definition: Relating to war.
Relating to armies or ground forces.
A word or phrase which has recently been coined; a new word or phrase.
The act or instance of coining, or uttering a new word.
The newly coined, meaningless words or phrases of someone with a psychosis, usually schizophrenia.
The introduction of new doctrine, for example in theology.
To conquer, defeat.
To reach some destination or object, despite difficulty or toil (now usually intransitive, with preposition or locative adverb).
To triumph or achieve victory in (a game, a war, etc.).
To gain (a prize) by succeeding in competition or contest.
definitions.example: to win the jackpot in a lottery;  to win a bottle of wine in a raffle

definitions.definition: To obtain (someone) by wooing; to make an ally or friend of (frequently with over).
To achieve victory.
definitions.example: Who would win in a fight between an octopus and a dolphin?

definitions.definition: To have power, coercion or control.
definitions.example: Ever since the 2013 Boston Marathon bombing, Bostonians now run as "One Boston." The terrorists did not win.

definitions.definition: To obtain (something desired).
definitions.example: The company hopes to win an order from the government worth over 5 million dollars.

definitions.definition: To cause a victory for someone.
definitions.example: The policy success should win the elections for Mr. Smith.

definitions.definition: To extract (ore, coal, etc.).
Freedom from some business or activity.
Free time given over to a specific purpose; occupation, activity.
A period during which official activity or business is formally suspended; an official holiday from university, law courts etc.
A holiday; a stretch of leisure time away from work or duty and devoted to rest or pleasure.
The act of vacating something; moving out.
definitions.example: The Conservative Party’s vacation of the centre ground gave an opportunity to its opponents.

definitions.definition: The act of making legally void.
To spend or take a vacation.

A mill worked by persons treading upon steps on the periphery of a wide wheel having a horizontal axis. It is used principally as a means of prison discipline.
A mill worked by horses, dogs, etc., treading an endless belt.
Any of various dark-coloured Asian species of papilionid butterflies of the genus Papilio.
To intentionally cause the destruction of.
To self-destruct.
The operation of deleting data.
To remove markings or information
definitions.example: I erased that note because it was wrong.

definitions.definition: To obliterate information from (a storage medium), such as to clear or (with magnetic storage) to demagnetize.
definitions.example: I'm going to erase this tape.

definitions.definition: To obliterate (information) from a storage medium, such as to clear or to overwrite.
definitions.example: I'm going to erase those files.

definitions.definition: To remove a runner from the bases via a double play or pick off play
definitions.example: Jones was erased by a 6-4-3 double play.

definitions.definition: To be erased (have markings removed, have information removed or be cleared of information).
definitions.example: The chalkboard erased easily.

definitions.definition: To disregard (a group, an orientation, etc.); to prevent from having an active role in society.
To kill; assassinate.
The large, usually yellowish-green or black, pulpy fruit of the avocado tree.
The avocado tree, Persea americana, of the laurel family.
A dark chartreuse colour, like the colour of the skin of an avocado.
Of a dull yellowish-green colour.
Any one out of an indefinite number of persons; anyone; any person.
definitions.example: Anybody will do.

definitions.definition: A person of some consideration or standing.
definitions.example: Everybody who wants to be anybody will come to Jake's party.

definitions.definition: A person who is knowledgeable of, or who collects antiques; an antiquarian.
An aficionado or student of antiquities, ancient artifacts, historic sites, ancient writings, or things of the past.
Relating to antiquity.
A designated player that attempts to prevent the opposing team from scoring by protecting a goal.
An unbroken view of an entire surrounding area.
A picture or series of pictures representing a continuous scene.
A comprehensive survey.
A risky or daring undertaking or journey.
An event that is not, or cannot be, foreseen.
The thing risked; especially, something sent to sea in trade.
A contract which binds a person to work for another, under specified conditions, for a specified time (often as an apprentice).
A document, written as duplicates separated by indentations, specifying such a contract.
An indentation.
To bind a person under such a contract.
To indent; to make hollows, notches, or wrinkles in; to furrow.
definitions.example: Though age may creep on, and indenture the brow.
